title: Physically validate HAL power telemetry on battery-capable boards
description: Why this exists: HAL power telemetry ultimately needs confirmation against real battery/PMIC hardware, which was out of scope for no-hardware t2hn.6.1 work. What needs to be done: after provider-backed readings exist, validate battery percent, voltage, charging, and external power behavior on at least one supported battery/PMIC board and record expected tolerances. Acceptance criteria: hardware test evidence identifies board, firmware build, observed values, charging/external-power transitions, and any driver limitations.
notes: No-hardware loop block 2026-07-02: acceptance requires unavailable physical hardware or real external endpoints (GNSS receivers/time behavior, battery/PMIC ADC telemetry, physical LCI firmware, real BLE app/phone/GATT policy, or board-level validation). No further software-only work is currently separable inside this Bead; resume with physical devices/endpoints available or split a new no-hardware child if a specific simulator/test-harness gap is discovered.
